title: New substances: living organisms - Canada.ca
content:
## New substances: Living organisms

## New substances: Organisms
The New Substances Notification Regulations (Organisms) [NSNR (Organisms)] help protect Canadians and the environment allowing for the assessment of new living organisms prior to their introduction into the Canadian marketplace.
If you plan to manufacture or import a new living organism subject to notification under the NSNR (Organisms), you are required to provide information to Environment and Climate Change Canada and Health Canada.

## Services and information

### Overview, guidance, fact sheets, and technical frameworks
Information and guidance on the NSNR (Organisms), including the notification, testing, and risk assessment of new living organisms.

### New Substances Notification Regulation (Organisms)
Full text of NSNR (Organisms), amendments and consultations on the registry of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 .
Advisory notes clarify certain aspects of the New Substances program for the benefit of individuals responsible for complying with the NSNR (Organisms).

### Consultations on certain living organisms new to Canada
Consultations supporting risk assessments of certain new living organisms, such as vertebrate animals, prescribed living organisms and groups of living organisms.

### Consultation and Engagement: NSNR (Organisms) Regulatory amendment
Supporting documents used during engagement with stakeholders on the modernization of NSNR (Organisms)

### Risk assessment summaries for living organisms
Summaries of human health and environmental risk assessments of new living organisms

### New substances screening assessments: micro-organisms
General Information on the Screening Assessments of Micro-organisms
